line: Implemented emotional-trajectory recall/dashboard integration by surfacing bounded affect history in canonical health output and wiring health/dashboard drill-downs to `mood_history`.

items: `crates/membrain-core/src/health.rs`
added `affect_history_rows`, `latest_affect_snapshot`, `latest_affect_tick` to `BrainHealthInputs` and `BrainHealthReport`
added `DashboardViewId::AffectTrajectory` with stable snake_case serialization

line: - added `affect_trajectory` dashboard card and `/mood_history` drill-down path

items: added `affect_trajectory` dashboard card and `/mood_history` drill-down path

line: - extended memory subsystem metrics/detail with affect-history counters

items: extended memory subsystem metrics/detail with affect-history counters
`crates/membrain-daemon/src/daemon.rs`

line: - health report now derives affect-history summary from stored `mood_history(...)`

items: health report now derives affect-history summary from stored `mood_history(...)`
`crates/membrain-core/tests/recall_pipeline_integration.rs`

line: - updated health surface test inputs/assertions for affect trajectory summary and drill-down

items: updated health surface test inputs/assertions for affect trajectory summary and drill-down
`crates/membrain-daemon/tests/runtime_share_unshare_parity.rs`

line: - added parity assertion that daemon `health` exposes the affect trajectory dashboard/drill-down alongside `mood_history`

items: added parity assertion that daemon `health` exposes the affect trajectory dashboard/drill-down alongside `mood_history`
`docs/CLI.md`
`docs/MCP_API.md`
`docs/OPERATIONS.md`

line: - The existing recall integration was already present through daemon-side `mood_congruent` use of `mood_history`; the missing user-facing value was canonical health/dashboard surfacing.

items: The existing recall integration was already present through daemon-side `mood_congruent` use of `mood_history`; the missing user-facing value was canonical health/dashboard surfacing.

line: - `DashboardViewId` needed serde snake_case to match existing machine-readable view expectations in daemon JSON.

items: `DashboardViewId` needed serde snake_case to match existing machine-readable view expectations in daemon JSON.

line: - Kept `mood_history` as the detailed read-only history surface rather than inventing a second history API.

items: Kept `mood_history` as the detailed read-only history surface rather than inventing a second history API.

line: - Used the health/dashboard path as the “recall integration” surface by exposing the same bounded affect snapshot that recall-facing mood congruence depends on.

items: Used the health/dashboard path as the “recall integration” surface by exposing the same bounded affect snapshot that recall-facing mood congruence depends on.

line: - Limited the patch to health/report/doc/parity seams instead of refactoring the broader recall engine.

items: Limited the patch to health/report/doc/parity seams instead of refactoring the broader recall engine.

line: - `latest_affect_tick` currently resolves from stored trajectory row `tick_end`; in daemon health this may still be `null`/`0` depending on how the row is recorded, though the bounded snapshot and row count are surfaced correctly.

items: `latest_affect_tick` currently resolves from stored trajectory row `tick_end`; in daemon health this may still be `null`/`0` depending on how the row is recorded, though the bounded snapshot and row count are surfaced correctly.

line: - File reservations reported `released: 0` at the end even though the work completed; if needed, another agent can re-check MCP reservation state.

items: File reservations reported `released: 0` at the end even though the work completed; if needed, another agent can re-check MCP reservation state.
